title: Wallet balances can be corrupted when transaction writes fail or race
reasoning: Transaction.save mutates and saves wallet balances before saving the transaction itself, and delete mutates the wallet before deleting the transaction. These operations are not wrapped in a database transaction and do not lock or use atomic F-expression updates. If the transaction save/delete fails after the wallet save, the wallet balance no longer matches persisted transactions. Concurrent creates/edits/deletes against the same wallet can also overwrite each other's balance calculations because each request reads and writes a stale Wallet.balance value in Python.
recommendation: Wrap transaction and wallet updates in django.db.transaction.atomic(), lock affected wallet and old transaction rows with select_for_update(), and update balances with F expressions or recompute from transactions after commit-sensitive changes.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Finances/tests.py contains only the generated placeholder and no tests for transaction save/delete side effects, failure rollback, or concurrent balance updates.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests that simulate a failed Transaction.save after wallet mutation and concurrent transaction creation/editing for the same wallet, asserting the final Wallet.balance equals the sum of persisted transactions.
minimumFixScope: Transaction.save and Transaction.delete balance mutation logic.
